success_surface: Merged cell handling combined with unique value counting across multiple worksheet ranges
effective_behaviors: Conduct pre-flight inspection to identify structural elements (merged cells, sheet layout, data types) before modifying
Perform structural changes (unmerge, reorder columns) before data extraction or counting operations
Create reusable helper functions for repetitive counting/filtering logic across different ranges
Use explicit cell-by-cell iteration via row/column indices when range slicing causes iteration errors
Add verification logging after modifications to confirm expected output values were written
evidence: Agent ran inspection code to discover 3 merged cell ranges before starting manipulation
Unmerged cells first, then counted unique values - ensuring no address conflicts
First attempt using ws['E2:E10'] returned tuples; agent pivoted to ws.cell(row=row, column=5) pattern
Count unique function was abstracted into count_unique_items_in_range() to reuse across 3 target ranges
Print statements verified H10/H20/H24 contained correct counts (9, 9, 4) after save

memory_items.title: Structural Pre-Check Before Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Always scan for merged cells, hidden rows/sheets, and existing formulas before modifying content.
memory_items.generalization: Run ws.merged_cells.ranges inspection and list sheets programmatically prior to any write operations to avoid addressing conflicts.

memory_items.title: Unmerge First, Then Count
memory_items.lesson: If cell merging affects the target range, unmerge before extracting values or counting unique entries.
memory_items.generalization: Complete all structural changes (unmerge, insert, delete) before performing aggregation, filtering, or value counting on affected regions.

memory_items.title: Cell Access Fallback Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When ws['range'] slicing returns unexpected tuple structures, fall back to ws.cell(row=X, column=Y) iteration.
memory_items.generalization: For single-column aggregations, prefer row/column indexed cell access over range slicing to avoid nested tuple unpacking issues.

memory_items.title: Verification After Write
memory_items.lesson: After saving modified files, verify critical cells contain expected values before confirming completion.
memory_items.generalization: Include post-save readback of modified cell positions in execution logs to catch silent failures or overwrite issues.

edits.content:
## 6. Structural Change Sequencing
- **Merge Handling**: Scan for merged cells (`ws.merged_cells.ranges`) before data operations. If merging overlaps target cells, unmerge before counting/extracting values.
- **Order Priority**: Complete all structural changes (unmerge, hide/show, insert/delete) before aggregations or value counting on affected areas.
- **Access Method**: For single-column iterations, use `cell(row, column)` indexing rather than range slicing (`ws['A1:A10']`) to avoid nested tuple unpacking errors.
- **Write Verification**: Read back modified cells after saving to confirm values were written correctly (especially for aggregated/calculated outputs).
